title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary: No blocking changes requested. The reviewer flagged two pre-existing issues in sibling tests (not this PR): hardcoded replica counts that should be dynamic, and insufficient timeout margins. Both are suggested as follow-up work.

## INFO (2)

### I-001: Sibling tests hardcode replica counts (pre-existing)
**File**: `chainsaw-test.yaml` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Derive expected values from source of truth instead of hardcoding

**Problem**: The memcached test correctly reads the desired replica count from .spec.replicas, but sibling tests operator-pod-crash and api-pod-kill-pdb still hardcode their replica counts (2 and 3 respectively). This PR improves on the existing pattern but the sibling tests remain inconsistent.

**Code**:
```
N/A — pre-existing issue in sibling test files, not in this PR's diff
```

**Action Required**: Consider backporting the dynamic replica count approach to the sibling tests operator-pod-crash and api-pod-kill-pdb in a follow-up PR.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
In sibling tests, replace hardcoded replica counts with dynamic reads from .spec.replicas, following the pattern established in this PR.
```

**Related**: I-002

---

### I-002: Sibling test timeout budgets are tighter than this test (pre-existing)
**File**: `chainsaw-test.yaml` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Test script timeout must exceed worst-case loop duration

**Problem**: Sibling tests have insufficient timeout margins: operator-pod-crash has a 150s timeout for a 240s worst case, and api-pod-kill-pdb has a 120s timeout for a 120s worst case (zero margin). This PR correctly sizes its timeout at 150s for a 120s worst case (30s margin), setting a good precedent.

**Code**:
```
N/A — pre-existing issue in sibling test files, not in this PR's diff
```

**Action Required**: Consider reviewing and adjusting timeout budgets in sibling tests operator-pod-crash and api-pod-kill-pdb to ensure adequate margin above worst-case loop duration.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Adjust sibling test timeouts to include at least a 25% margin above worst-case script duration, following the precedent set by this PR.
```

**Related**: I-001

---

## Summary

This is a well-executed bug fix. The PR correctly replaces a fragile Pod-level kubectl wait with Deployment-level readyReplicas polling that is resilient to pod replacement, dynamically reads the desired replica count from the Deployment spec, properly calculates timeout budgets with margin, and updates documentation to match. No issues were found against this PR — the two INFO findings are pre-existing issues in sibling tests noted for future improvement.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: Merge without changes. The PR is clean, well-structured, and follows established project patterns. The two INFO findings are pre-existing issues in sibling tests and do not affect this PR.
resolution_summary: Both review comments (I-001 and I-002) are INFO-level observations about pre-existing issues in sibling test files (operator-pod-crash and api-pod-kill-pdb), not actionable feedback for this PR's code. No changes were required because this PR already implements the correct patterns that the reviewer wants backported: dynamic replica counts read from .spec.replicas (I-001) and properly sized timeout budgets with 30s margin (I-002). The reviewer recommends follow-up PRs to bring the sibling tests in line with the patterns established here.
